---
title: "Artificial Intelligence"
page-layout: full
aliases:
  - ai
format:
  html:
    css: ../assets/css/topic-layout.css
---

::: {.content-container}
::: {.topic-intro .ai-topic}
Large language models, prompt engineering, AI agents, and practical AI applications.
:::


::: {.subtopics-grid .ai-topic}


::: {.topic .collapsed}
### Language Models
Understanding how large language models work and how to use them effectively.

::: {.subtopics}
#### Discursive Indexing
How names, genres, and cultural labels function as control handles over learned distributions.

[Discursive indexing of latent distributions and cultural compression as model control.](discursive-indexing)

#### Prompt Engineering
Techniques for eliciting desired behaviors from language models.

#### Fine-tuning and Adaptation
Methods for adapting pre-trained models to specific tasks.
:::
:::


::: {.topic .collapsed}
### AI Agents
Building autonomous systems that use language models as reasoning engines.

::: {.subtopics}
#### Agent Architectures
Design patterns for AI agents: ReAct, tool use, planning, and memory.

#### Tool Integration
Connecting language models to external systems and APIs.

#### Multi-Agent Systems
Coordination and collaboration between multiple AI agents.
:::
:::


::: {.topic .collapsed}
### Applications
Practical applications of AI in engineering and software development.

::: {.subtopics}
#### Code Generation
Using AI for writing, reviewing, and refactoring code.

#### Knowledge Management
AI-assisted organization and retrieval of information.

#### Creative Applications
AI for writing, design, and content generation.
:::
:::

:::
:::

<script>
window.addEventListener('load', function() {
    document.querySelectorAll('.topic').forEach(topic => {
        const header = topic.querySelector('h3');
        header.addEventListener('click', () => {
            topic.classList.toggle('collapsed');
        });
    });
});
</script>
